person.name: Margery

burialPlace.address: St. Andrew's Churchyard Wiveliscombe, Taunton Deane Borough, Somerset, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: Margaret Bennett "wife of John Bennett," was buried in Wiveliscombe in 1564. John Bennett is described as a tanner. For this reason, John Bennett Boddie speculated in 1938 that for this reason that he was the father of another tanner, Robert Bennett whose will was probated in Wiveliscombe in 1603. No relationship has been proven. What's stated here is all we know about the couple.

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Margery had at least 1 son with John Bennett. She died in 1564, in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, and was buried in St Andrew's Church, Burnham-on-Sea, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St. Andrew's Churchyard Wiveliscombe, Ta: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 번함--시 스트 앤드루'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Margery(은)는 John Bennett(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1564년 영국 서머세트 위벨리스코브에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 번함--시 스트 앤드루'스 처르치에 묻혔다.
